title: Bridge Essay: Study of immediate neurological and autonomic changes during kapalbhati pranayam

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Breath and soul — pranayama, breath-brain coupling, respiratory-brain interaction, cardiorespiratory entrainment, vagal pathways of consciousness.

**Study of immediate neurological and autonomic changes during kapalbhati pranayama in yoga practitioners.** — J Family Med Prim Care. PMID: 35360798. DOI: 10.4103/jfmpc.jfmpc_1662_21. Kapalbhati is afast pace respiratory exercise or pranayam, which is supposed to be practiced by yogis to clean their brain. Pranayamas are well known to improve heart rate variability (HRV) ultimately leading to better autonomic functions. Other studies have observed the immediate effect of kapalbhati on various neurological (brain and spine) and autonomic functions, but their results are varied and inconclusive.
